titleStmt.title: Letter from Sir Wilfrid Spender to his wife Lady Lillian Spender, 11 July 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Wilfrid Spender

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: In this letter from Wilfrid Spender to his wife, Lillian, Spender gives an account of his departure from the Ulster Division - his current location being the HQ of the 2nd Army. He expects to be given a training position, and expresses dismay at the thought that three quarters of the new recruits will be from Derby: a step down after the men of Ulster, whose equal was not 'ever seen in the history of mankind'. Spender expresses concern that the representation of the Ulster Division by newspapers has changed from lack of interest to 'gush'. He finishes with a brief description of the local countryside. This letter is part of a series of correspondence from Sir Wilfrid Bliss Spender to his wife Lady Lillian Spender (née Dean, 1880-1968). The letters date mainly from 1915 to 1918, when Sir Wilfrid was serving in France. The letters concern politics, the Irish question, personal life, and Wilfrid's experience of the Great War.Sir Wilfrid Spender (1876 - 1960) was a member of the British Army until 1913 when controversy surrounding his signature of the Ulster Solemn League and Covenant resulted in his resignation. Spender was called up for military service in 1914 and from 1915 served in the 36th (Ulster) Division. Throughout the war, Spender remained in contact with the Ulster Unionist leadership, and consistently opposed Home Rule and the six-county solution.

msIdentifier.repository: Public Record Office of Northern Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
July 11 1916 My pr I've been up since before 5
motoring over the country with S,
a journey which the pr would have
enjoyed far more than a BW. We
went behind the greater part of this
British front seeing the smoke of this
big battle early on then coming
down hill to the flat plains later. I went to H.Q of the 2nd army
seeing Turner who is GS01 &
who was a fellow subaltern at
Woolwich with me nearly 10 20 30
years ago. He was friendly without
being cordial. Tonight I am stopping temporarily
in a chateau more English than
any I have seen with a French lady
not unlike Mol as hostess.
We hope expect to go very shortly a
little way away to a training area
at which we shall I suppose get our
drafts, but one 's heart fail one a little
at, making up our battalions with
3/4 of Derby recruits. The Ulster Div-
ision which I can think fairly
claim to have helped train can never
be the same again. I doubt if its
equal will ever be seen in the
history of mankind. The newspaper reticence has
been filled with changed into something approaching gush & I am a little afraid of
its being over done. I also criticise
the inspiration of their fulsome account
in the DM & the Times. Are they
intended to distract attention from
other matters, or is their courses
quickened. I'm so sleepy that I can
hardly stay wake, & I'm
without my maid to inspire
me. S who often talks rather
poor wanted to go to a
big hotel but I absolutely
declined & much prefer my
chateau billet with Douglas
to look after me, & I
persuaded him to do the like. I wish I was staying
here as the country is nice not
unlike Norfolk whilst what we
go to on the 13th is likely to
be like Cambridgeshire only much
less industry. To bed
Your bw WB Spender
